summary: The blood-brain barrier is typically present and only absent at select regions known collectively termed 'circumventricular organs.' Neurohypophysis uniquely lacks this protective feature within these specialized structures; hence D represents an accurate choice for absence while others do not fit such a description precisely based provided information. Agent1 concluded by identifying which structure does NOT lack the BBB despite being considered under similar circumstances due to its unique anatomical characteristics related specifically towards endocrine function rather than vascular protection mechanisms typical elsewhere outside central nervous system boundaries maintained otherwise consistently through most cerebral tissues examined clinically or pathologically thus far documented scientifically concerning human physiology involving neurological disorders affecting intracranial compartments protected primarily via tight junction
